Putrid Warrior: Solid filler body in the worst color pair (WB) — the 55.5 GIH WR is propped up by white's strength, not this card. Fine if your Orzhov deck wants curve, but never a priority pick.
Ixidor, Reality Sculptor: No GIH data means it saw too little serious play to trust — the cloak/disguise face-down payoff is a build-around in a format where build-arounds are mostly dead. Speculative at best; don't draft your deck around it.
Goblin Warchief: D- grade and a non-tribal home in a format with no Goblin density makes this an off-color trap. Even in Izzet/Gruul it does little; sideboard/unplayable.
Mentor of the Meek: CGB's B+ wildly overrates this — 52% GIH and negative IWD say it's barely a role-player. A card-advantage engine that needs a wide, cheap-creature white deck (Boros/Selesnya) to fire; otherwise filler.
Absolving Lammasu: Middling 53% flyer-ish lifegain body — playable white filler that goes around pick 6. Fine in any white deck (white's the best color), but never exciting; take removal and bombs first.
Essence of Antiquity: Just-above-water defensive golem; the -3.4 IWD shows it's a deck you don't miss. Pure speed-bump filler for grindy white control shells, not a card you prioritize.
No Witnesses: Experts split, and the data sides with caution — 52% GIH and the Clue-refund clause make this a 'win-more' wrath that often just trades a card and arms the opponent. Strong only as a true board-reset in midrange mirrors; don't overdraft the rare tag.
Benthic Criminologists: LR's 'just bad' read holds up against the deck — 53% GIH but a paltry 17% play rate says people correctly cut it. A clunky top-end clue-sac body; only a late-curve filler in a dedicated artifact/clue Dimir-Simic pile.
Deduce: Better than its C grade — 55.4 GIH with positive IWD on a 2-mana cantrip-plus-Clue is excellent glue for any blue deck. Premium for control/clue shells; smooths draws and feeds investigate payoffs.
Lost in the Maze: The 58.2 GD_WR confirms Numot — a one-mana combat trick/protection enchantment that overperforms in grindy blue decks. Strong playable, but the ward-2 disguise creatures dodge it, so it's slightly worse than raw numbers late.
Basilica Stalker: Replaceable black common; 52% GIH is below the curve and black is the worst color. A serviceable evasive/detective body for Dimir-Orzhov, but pure filler you can wheel.
Long Goodbye: Black's best removal — cheap, uncounterable, and brutal against the format's cheap disguise flips, with a 56.8 GIH and strong IWD to prove it. High pick within black, but the MV-4+ blind spot and black's weakness mean don't commit to black early just for it.
Slimy Dualleech: Quietly good drain-y black uncommon at 53.8 GIH with positive IWD — a solid playable that grinds well in Orzhov/Rakdos. Take it as a reliable role-player, not a bomb.
The Chase Is On: A sneaky-strong red combat trick at 55.4 GIH that punishes blocks and enables aggression — better than its C grade. Great in Boros/Gruul tempo decks where you're attacking; weaker in grindy shells.
Galvanize: The experts are right to deflate the hype — 56.4 GIH is good but the four-mana cost to kill ward-2 disguise creatures makes it merely solid, not premium. Strong red playable that's a notch below efficient removal; Shock would be better.
Pyrotechnic Performer: Genuine bomb — 59.8 GIH, 1.8 ALSA, near-universal play. LSV's best-red-by-winrate read is fully backed by the data; a premium first-pick that pushes you into red and rewards spell density.
Axebane Ferox: Premium green bomb: a 4/4 deathtouch haste with built-in ward (Collect Evidence 4) that's often unkillable, at 59.2 GIH and pick 1-2 ALSA. First-pick quality, especially strong in Simic/Golgari where evidence is plentiful.
Hedge Whisperer: Fine green ramp/detective body at 53.4 GIH — a useful mana-smoother and investigate enabler for grindy Simic/Selesnya, but flat IWD marks it as a role-player, not a priority.
Slime Against Humanity: The trap LR warned about — 44.4 GIH is a disaster. A beloved build-around that does nothing without a critical mass of named copies, which you'll never assemble in draft. Hard pass.
Blood Spatter Analysis: Underrated Rakdos engine at 56.5 GIH with strong IWD — a repeatable removal-and-value enchantment that gives the format's worst guild (Rakdos) a real reason to exist. Strong playable when you're in BR aggro/midrange.
Ezrim, Agency Chief: Format-defining bomb — 63.1 GIH and a staggering +10.9 IWD make this near-unbeatable once online. The single best card in this batch; premier first-pick that draws you into elite Azorius, with a clue/artifact engine baked in.
Kylox's Voltstrider: Strong Izzet payoff at 56% GIH — a fast, hard-to-block Vehicle that rewards a spell-heavy UR tempo build. Take it highly when in Izzet, but its value dips without enough spells/creatures to crew and trigger it.
Repulsive Mutation: Top-tier Simic gold uncommon — pump-plus-counter at 59.8 GIH and +5.8 IWD is a two-for-one blowout. Premium pick in GU/GW; protects your threats and wins combat or the stack.
Urgent Necropsy: Flexible Golgari removal with a huge +7.8 IWD — destroys nearly anything and 'at worst gets a creature.' The lower OH_WR (54.9) flags it as awkward early, but it's a strong playable that pulls weight in any BG evidence shell.
Gravestone Strider: Numot's underrated-all-star read is real — colorless fixing plus graveyard hate that self-exiles cleanly, playable in any deck at 53.4 GIH. Solid filler everywhere, genuinely good in Golgari/graveyard-matters builds.
Meticulous Archive: A clean Azorius dual land — the 56.7 OH_WR shows it smooths early draws, exactly what a two-color guild format wants. Strong supporting pick for WU; fixing without a real cost.
Case of the Gorgon's Kiss: The 48.8 GIH and 20% play rate confirm this Case underdelivers — too slow and conditional to 'solve' reliably in a grindy meta. A niche removal-ish enchantment for dedicated black control; mostly sideboard.
Krosan Tusker: A 56.5% GIH WR on a card that's basically a cantrip-cycler stapled to a 6/5 fixes mana AND smooths draws — quietly excellent glue for any green midrange deck. Goes around pick 5 but pulls way above its weight; grab it.
Duskmantle, House of Shadow: Zero-data tap-land for the worst color pair (Dimir). In a grindy die-roll format the tempo loss isn't worth a marginal late-game mana sink. Sideboard/cut.
High Alert: A dead build-around in a format where build-arounds are graveyard-bound. No play data and a D- speaks for itself — you're not assembling enough high-toughness creatures to make a Doran enchantment matter. Unplayable.
Ghostly Prison: Mythic name, 51.3% reality. Purely reactive Pillow Fort in a proactive white-aggro metagame — it does nothing the turn it lands and white wants to be attacking. Niche sideboard tech vs aggro, not a maindeck card.
Assemble the Players: 47.9% GIH and a brutal -7 IWD: this brick is the trap the rare slot warns you about. Even a disguise-dense deck whiffs into lands too often. Leave it; play a real card.
Forum Familiar: Flat 50% — pure filler that needs a fragile combat-trick trigger to matter. In white's aggressive shells you'd rather have a card that does something on its own. Late-table playable at best, first cut from a good deck.
Not on My Watch: Don't trust the 55.5% here — it's inflated by being run in winning aggressive decks. As LoL flags, the timing fights white's proactive plan; it's a reactive fog-removal hybrid. Fine as a 22nd card, not a pick you prioritize.
Bubble Smuggler: Disguise body that cloaks something on death — a 53% role-player that smooths your face-down density in Simic/blue tempo. Replaceable filler; serviceable but never exciting.
Dramatic Accusation: Suspect-on-an-aura is removal-lite (can't block + repeatable tap) and the 55.3% GD WR shows it grinds fine once you're ahead. Numot's cooling is fair — it's solid filler, not a high pick, best in controlling blue decks.
Mistway Spy: A 52% evasive disguise body — competent curve-filler for blue tempo decks that want cheap face-down attackers. Nothing special; a mid-pack playable you're happy to run but won't reach for.
Case of the Stashed Skeleton: 57% GIH and a free tutor make this look premium, but heed Numot's end-of-format reversal: the 2/1 can't block in a get-on-board format. Still a strong playable for the card you fetch, just not the bomb the grade suggests.
Macabre Reconstruction: 35% play rate tells the story — a clunky reanimation/recursion sorcery that only the grindiest black decks want. 51.7% is replacement-level. Sideboard filler for value mirrors, not a maindeck staple.
Snarling Gorehound: The 54% and strong 57% OH WR reward you early, but Numot's right that the power-2-or-less trigger underfires. A fine aggressive two-drop in black decks; play it on curve, don't overrate the card draw.
Concealed Weapon: 51% looks meh but this is archetype-dependent: it's a token-deck enabler that turns a Satchel 1/1 flyer into a 4-power clock. Mediocre in a vacuum, genuinely strong in red/white go-wide and flyers shells.
Gearbane Orangutan: Replacement-level 52% red common with marginal artifact-hate upside. Fine body, fills a curve slot, occasionally blows up a Clue or equipment. Pure filler — last cards in the deck.
Reckless Detective: Believe the 58.6% and elite 62.8% OH WR — this 0/3 walls early aggression then converts Clues into free cards on the attack. A premium common-slot two-drop and the nut-curve partner to Novice Inspector; high pick in any clue deck.
Bite Down on Crime: 57.2% with a 58.9% GD WR vindicates the upward re-eval: removal that ignores ward 2 and pays you off via collect evidence. Strong green playable; even light CE density makes it shine. Prioritize in any green deck.
Hide in Plain Sight: 63% GIH and a 9.6 IWD — this is a genuine bomb-tier green rare, a Collected Company that never misses. First-pick caliber; two ward-2 bodies on the floor and a real haymaker on the ceiling. Take it over almost anything.
They Went This Way: 54% but a 48% play rate — a setup-y green token sorcery that only the right go-wide deck wants. Solid in Selesnya/ramp shells with payoffs, unexciting elsewhere. Speculative mid-pack pick, not a staple.
Buried in the Garden: 59.7% premium uncommon: catch-all O-Ring removal that ALSO ramps you — the card that makes Selesnya worth drafting. Hits anything, advances your board. High pick and a strong signal to be in GW.
Faerie Snoop: Don't overdraft it despite the 54% — LoL's right that Snoops are mana-hungry with diminishing returns and clog tempo Detectives. One is great value, the third is a liability. Good role-player, not a build-around magnet.
Lazav, Wearer of Faces: 57.6% and a 7.3 IWD make this a real Dimir payoff — evasive, grows, and recurs threats in a grindy color pair that needs the help. Strong UB rare; the kind of card that justifies the format's worst guild.
Riftburst Hellion: 53.6% Gruul filler — a big disguise body that's fine on curve but does little to swing a game. Replacement-level beef for RG; play it, don't prioritize it.
Vannifar, Evolved Enigma: 61.2% GIH, 1.7 ALSA — a format-defining mythic that flickers a free cloak every combat and snowballs out of control. Premier first-pick that locks you green; both Numot and LR call it absurd and the data agrees. Take it, no question.
Lumbering Laundry: 9.9% play rate and 51% — a clunky colorless beater nobody wants. Only relevant if you're scraping a fixing-light deck for a top-end body. Effectively a sideboard card.
Public Thoroughfare: Both experts and the 52% agree: a taxing tap-land is a real cost in a die-roll format. Only justifiable as fixing in a true three-color build, which MKM rarely wants. Avoid unless your mana forces it.
Clandestine Meddler: 52.9% replacement-level black uncommon — a fine evasive body with marginal upside that grinds okay but never dominates. Mid-pack filler for black decks; first cut when your pool improves.
Mass Hysteria: No-data F enchantment that symmetrically gives ALL creatures haste — pure build-around chaff that helps your opponent in a grindy midrange format. Sideboard/unplayable; never main-deck it.
Worldspine Wurm: 26% play rate is desperation ramp inclusion, not a real signal — a 15-mana wurm in a 7/10-speed grindy format is a non-starter. D- and effectively unplayable outside a hard Simic/green ramp pile.
Jace, Wielder of Mysteries: 56.6% GIH and a 2.7 ALSA mark a legit blue bomb planeswalker: ticks toward card advantage and protects itself, fitting any controlling blue midrange shell. Strong first-pick, especially in Simic or Azorius grind.
Fabricate: 3.3% play rate and an F say it all — a narrow tutor with no artifact density to fetch in Limited. Sideboard/unplayable; wheels every time.
Aurelia's Vindicator: The data screams bomb: 68.6% GIH, 14.1 IWD, 1.2 ALSA — the best card in the set. Disguise hides its cost then flips to exile multiple creatures, and white being the top color makes it an auto-P1P1 every time.
Griffnaut Tracker: Negative IWD and 51.6% GIH expose a vanilla-ish body that does little when you're behind — a curve-filler, not a payoff. Playable role-player in white aggressive decks, nothing more; cuttable when your two-drops are deep.
Novice Inspector: 59.5% GIH on a one-drop is absurd — Thraben Inspector reprint that bodies early, chumps late, and leaves a Clue. The format's best common; splashable, never cut, and a near-auto P1P1 in any white deck.
Burden of Proof: 52.7% GIH and negative IWD flag a tempo-positive but card-disadvantageous aura that whiffs to removal — fine in a tempo-y blue deck (Azorius/Simic) where the cloak/clue upside lands. Solid filler, not a high pick.
Eliminate the Impossible: 54.2% with a strong 55.6 GD WR shows a flexible blue instant that rewards a built board — better from ahead than as a topdeck. Solid playable interaction in any blue deck, best where you can leverage tempo.
Out Cold: 55.3% GIH masks the real ceiling — the experts are right that this is a Simic/blue-tempo all-star you want MULTIPLES of, taps two and investigates uncounterably. Strong playable in blue tempo, mediocre filler elsewhere; prioritize in GU.
Cerebral Confiscation: Early-hype trap that fell off hard: 48.3% GIH and a 7.7 ALSA confirm the self-corrected misread. Black is the worst color and this is a marginal discard — sideboard/unplayable.
Massacre Girl, Known Killer: 56.7% GIH and a 1.5 ALSA make her a genuine black bomb that punishes both blocking and removal via wither — the rare black card worth forcing. Strong first-pick that pulls you into Rakdos/Golgari grind.
Soul Enervation: 54.3% with a healthy 56.0 GD WR — cheap, repeatable black interaction plus incremental drain that grinds out long games. Solid playable in Orzhov/Dimir; the kind of efficient removal-ish card a grindy format rewards.
Connecting the Dots: Rare case where the dumpster 47.4% GIH and -7.3 IWD are MORE right than the contrarian retro take — too much setup for a Rakdos build-around in the worst color pair. Sideboard/skip; let it wheel.
Goblin Maskmaker: 54.3% overall but a telling split — 58.7 OH vs 50.7 GD — confirms it's a disguise-density build-around that needs critical mass to shine. Archetype-only payoff in dedicated disguise/ramp red decks; otherwise mediocre filler.
Red Herring: The 56.8 OH vs 54.3 GD split tells the story — it's an aggro/sac-shell card (haste, replaces itself, sac fodder) that wants the Boros/Rakdos beatdown, not the average deck. Archetype-only; play multiples in dedicated aggro, cut elsewhere.
Case of the Locked Hothouse: 56.0% GIH with high variance baked in — a green ramp/payoff Case that's sometimes dead, sometimes unbeatable. Strong in Simic/Selesnya ramp shells with the curve to solve it; situational rare, not a slam.
A Killer Among Us: 60.2% GIH and 5.1 IWD vindicate the experts' upgrade — ~6 power across three bodies with a deathtouch mode is premium when even or ahead. Strong first-pick in green decks, fantastic in Selesnya/Simic go-wide.
Topiary Panther: 55.0% on a card that cycles, loads evidence, and grows off Roots — the 'every piece of the buffalo' overperformer. Solid-to-strong playable, premium specifically in Golgari where it feeds Collect Evidence; otherwise a smooth role-player.
Coerced to Kill: 58.8% GIH and 6.4 IWD make this a quiet Dimir all-star — steal-aura removal that swings the board by two creatures at once. Strong playable that elevates the otherwise-weak UB pair; prioritize it in Dimir.
Gadget Technician: 55.8% GIH on a flexible UR common — hard-cast as a 4 or disguised and flipped early into a thopter, fitting any Izzet/red artifact shell. Solid playable, the best of the disguise commons; takes early in UR.
Leyline of the Guildpact: 43.8% GIH and 14.2% play rate confirm Numot — a fun fixing trap, not a good card, since MKM is a tight 2-color guild format that doesn't want a 5c enabler. Unplayable; never draft it.
Rune-Brand Juggler: 54.9% GIH on a turn-2 menace body that loots — clean, aggressive Rakdos filler that smooths draws while attacking. Solid role-player in aggressive black-red shells, nothing you prioritize.
Warleader's Call: 59.8% GIH, 1.8 ALSA, and an A grade — an anthem-plus-burn payoff that's exactly what the flagship Boros go-wide deck wants. Premium first-pick in RW; one of the best uncommons-tier rares for the best deck in the format.
Magnetic Snuffler: 48.7% GIH and a 6.9 ALSA mark a clunky, overcosted artifact that the data rejects — 12% play rate says everyone agrees. Sideboard/unplayable outside a desperate artifact deck.
Raucous Theater: 54.7% GIH is dual-land inflation, not raw power — a Rakdos tapland that fixes and triggers surveil/landfall synergies. Take it for fixing in RB; useful but never over a real spell.
Torch the Witness: 59.9% GIH, 2.6 ALSA — scalable XR removal that investigates and slots into EVERY red deck on one pip. The experts' 'mythic uncommon of the format' call is right; premium removal, just remember it's sorcery-speed. High pick in any red.
Spell Snare: F-grade and 9.5% play for a reason: a CMC-2-only counter is too narrow for grindy MKM where threats span the curve. Constructed-only card; sideboard at absolute best.
Possibility Storm: Pure build-around chaos enchantment with no home in a clean two-color guild format. F-grade, near-zero play, unbuildable here. Hard pass.
Syr Konrad, the Grim: 54.4% GIH undersells how strong he plays in his shell: 56% GD and 85% play show he's a reliable drain engine when creatures die. Best in sacrifice-leaning Rakdos/Golgari, but black drags the home down a notch.
Show and Tell: Symmetrical mythic with no payoff to cheat out in limited; you have nothing big enough to justify gifting your opponent a free permanent. D-, basically unplayable.
Auspicious Arrival: Negative IWD is normal for a combat-trick-ish removal flicker; the 55%+ GIH across the board makes it a clean white role-player. Solid filler in any white deck, best where you have strong ETB bodies to blink.
Haazda Vigilante: Numot's hype outruns the data here: 54.8% GIH and a 5.7 ALSA say solid Boros top-end, not premium. A fine curve-topper for the aggressive white decks, not a card to prioritize.
On the Job: The experts are right and the early grades were wrong: this is a pseudo-Overrun that draws a Clue, 55.7% GIH despite a deceptively negative IWD. Premium go-wide payoff and a Boros/Selesnya win condition when you have ~17 creatures.
Candlestick: A 50.4% GIH Clue-equipment that's pure mediocrity; the 18% play rate confirms most drafters correctly leave it. Marginal filler only in a dedicated artifact/clue UR build.
Exit Specialist: 58.1% GD and 90% play make this a near-auto-include: tempo bounce that resets disguise investments plus a body and surveil. One of the format's best blue commons-slot uncommons; strong early pick in any blue deck.
Proft's Eidetic Memory: Elite signals across the board (58.9% GIH, 5.4 IWD, 2.4 ALSA) understate it; this is a premier first-pick that snowballs +1/+1 counters in any blue tempo deck, brilliant in Simic and Azorius. Take it over most rares.
Deadly Cover-Up: 59% GIH but read the room: this is a control-shell-only wrath that's a dead card in aggressive decks. Premium if your Dimir/control deck can survive to turn 5; never in a beatdown shell. Pick high only when committed to the grind.
Murder: The contested take resolves toward the data: 55% GIH, unconditional removal, just passive. Strong playable when black is open, but black is the worst color so it's a B-, not the bomb-answer it'd be in a better guild.
Toxin Analysis: A 53.9% trick that's a fine combat blowout with deathtouch/lifelink, but the 36% play rate shows it's deck-dependent. Solid role-player in black aggressive/disguise shells, not a maindeck staple.
Convenient Target: The -3.0 IWD is the tell: a suspect aura is removal-ish but hands the opponent menace, so it's a tempo wash. 52.4% GIH makes it playable Boros/Gruul filler, but don't overvalue it as real removal.
Harried Dronesmith: 52.2% GIH with high play but pedestrian output; the token-shuffling artificer is curve-fill, not a payoff. Solid filler two/three-drop in red aggressive decks, nothing to prioritize.
Rubblebelt Braggart: A 7.1 ALSA and 19.6% play say this vanilla-ish beater is near the bottom of red commons. Only a body for the most aggressive Boros/Gruul draws; otherwise leave it.
Case of the Trampled Garden: 53.5% GIH with strong 71% play; a cheap counter-engine Case that solves into a Giant Growth-style finisher. Good Selesnya/Simic role-player that rewards a wide board, solid mid-pick.
Loxodon Eavesdropper: 56.8% GIH and 57.5% GD make this a quietly excellent green common: a sizable body that investigates and pumps off clues. Strong playable in any green deck, better in clue-dense Simic/Selesnya builds.
Tunnel Tipster: Numot's right over LoL's lukewarm take: 58.8% OH and 90% play confirm a premium green two-drop that ramps and grows off face-down ETBs. Snap-pick early; one of green's best commons.
Crowd-Control Warden: 54.6% GIH gold common that's a sturdy body with relevant upside; reliable Selesnya glue. Solid playable that wheels less than its stats deserve, take it when in GW.
Gleaming Geardrake: The numbers scream bomb-uncommon: 60.3% GIH, 6.7 IWD, 61% OH. An evasive artifact that snowballs +1/+1 counters off artifacts/clues; a happy first-pick that anchors UR artifacts. Premium.
Lightning Helix: 58.9% GIH reprint that does it all: removal plus reach plus lifegain in the flagship Boros deck. Premium and color-committing; a strong first-pick that points you firmly RW.
Sanguine Savior: 54.1% GIH lifelink-flyer disguise body; a clean Boros/Orzhov closer with hidden combat upside. Solid playable gold common, not the top-tier card early hype suggested, but a fine mid-pick.
Wispdrinker Vampire: 55.6% GIH and 2.9 IWD reward a dedicated small-creature WB shell: a 2/4 flyer that drains and grants keywords on power-2-or-less ETBs. Strong archetype payoff, prioritize it when you're in the Orzhov go-low deck.
Magnifying Glass: 46.8% GIH and -4.4 IWD: a do-nothing mana-rock Clue that's actively bad in a tempo format. Sideboard/filler only for the most ramp-starved fixing-desperate decks.
Scene of the Crime: The retro 'unplayable' verdict is too harsh given 54.3% GIH and 77% play, but Numot's right it's just an 18th-land Clue, not fixing you build around. Fine inclusion as a free clue, low priority.
Break Out: 48.9% GIH and 7.3 ALSA confirm this disguise/reanimation enabler has no consistent home; it needs specific targets to be worth a card. Sideboard/unplayable RG build-around.
Ghost Quarter: F-grade trap: a colorless land that nets you nothing in a two-color guild format with few problem lands to blow up. Land-destruction is a luxury Limited rarely wants; leave it in the box. Unplayable.
Millstone: Mill is not a win condition in a grindy midrange format that ends games on the battlefield. A 4-mana do-nothing artifact; pure sideboard novelty. Skip it.
Ranger-Captain of Eos: Premium white bomb-ish two-drop: 58.9% GIH WR, 2.4 ALSA and a 61.9% OH WR confirm it dominates early. Fetches a one-drop and the silence ability protects your alpha strike. First-pick in any white deck, best in aggressive Boros/Orzhov.
Tragic Slip: Don't trust the B+ grade or the mythic frame: 51% GIH WR and -0.2 IWD say it's mediocre, since the -13 mode needs a creature to die first. Cheap insurance that's often a conditional -1/-1. Solid filler removal in any deck, not a high pick.
Call a Surprise Witness: 53.7% GIH WR with a negative IWD marks it as a deck-thinning value engine, not a tempo card — the WU/control build wants it, aggressive decks don't. Recur your best cheap creature or a Detective for repeated ETBs. Mid-pick role-player.
Inside Source: The data backs the hype and pushes past LR's cautious C+: 57.6% GIH WR and 92% play rate make this a near-auto-include. A 1/1 plus a 2/2 token plus a Detective anthem for three is a go-wide engine — strong playable, premium in white aggro (Boros/Orzhov).
Perimeter Enforcer: Premium uncommon two-drop — the experts and the 60.3% OH WR all agree. Evasive lifelink that snowballs off your Detective density; it punishes board stalls and races. P1-pickable in any white deck, best in Azorius/Boros. One of the format's best twos.
Case of the Filched Falcon: Hard archetype-only payoff: the 52.9% GIH WR is propped up entirely by UR Clue decks that can convert it into a 4/4 flyer. Without Clue density it's a dead enchantment. Speculative mid-pick that needs the Izzet artifacts deck to come together.
Fae Flight: Below-replacement aura: 51.8% GIH WR, negative IWD, and a sub-50% OH WR scream two-for-one risk in a removal-rich format. Flying plus ward only matters on an already-good creature. Sideboard/marginal — pass for nearly any creature.
Projektor Inspector: Quietly strong blue common: 56.1% GIH WR and 1.3 IWD on an evasive Detective that generates a Clue. Good body, good synergy fuel, fills the curve. Solid playable in any blue deck (Azorius/Dimir/Simic), better as your Detective count climbs.
Extract a Confession: Black's best common, full stop — 56.1% GIH WR and a huge 3.0 IWD. The edict cleanly bypasses ward 2 (the format's recurring problem) and the Collect Evidence cost ties into the graveyard plan. Premium black removal; high pick despite being a common.
Nightdrinker Moroii: The 53.1% GIH WR and sub-52% OH WR validate Ethan's low read over the hype — the lifeloss makes this an aggressive-only card that hurts you in the grind black usually wants. Playable beater in a fast Rakdos/Orzhov shell, mediocre elsewhere. Mid-pick.
Undercity Eliminator: Better than its 'just a 5-mana 3/3' rap: 54.5% GIH WR, 2.0 IWD, and a 56.2% gd WR show the deathtouch-ish removal ETB earns its slot. Ward 2 blunts the kill but it still trades up. Solid black role-player, fine in any B deck.
Cornered Crook: Reasonable red filler at 54.2% GIH WR — edges Undercity Eliminator since it can go face, matching the aggressive plan red wants. Neither is Flametongue (ward 2 limits the removal). Solid playable in Boros/Rakdos aggro, not a high pick.
Incinerator of the Guilty: The format's best uncommon-or-better red card and a true A bomb — 58.6% GIH WR, 5.2 IWD, 1.3 ALSA. A 6/6 flying trampler that wraths their board on connect via Collect Evidence ends games on its own. Slam it P1 every time you can cast it.
Shock: Best red common, and the experts are right that color flexibility makes it elite here: 57% GIH WR, instant-speed, and it kills disguise 2/2s for one mana. Premium efficient removal that keeps both red pairs open. High pick, never sideboard it out.
Chalk Outline: Archetype-only graveyard payoff: 51.9% GIH WR overall but Numot/LR rate it B/A in a built Golgari deck — the whole lane is just too slow for this aggressive format. Strong if you commit to the grindy GB engine, near-dead otherwise. Speculative late pick.
Nervous Gardener: Green's best early common and a key splash enabler: 56.7% GIH WR with a disguise body that fetches any basic. Free fixing in a guild format that loves clean splashes (red finishers, gold cards). Strong playable, prioritize it in Selesnya/Simic.
Undergrowth Recon: A 'healthy bad rare' — 2.5% play rate and no winning data because the lands-matter payoff has no support in MKM. Cute build-around with nothing to build around. Unplayable; cut on sight.
Curious Cadaver: The data sinks it: 50.3% GIH WR and a brutal 47.4% OH WR make this gold uncommon a near-trap in the format's worst color pair. Dimir is already dragged down by black; a slow recursive Detective doesn't fix that. Marginal — only if Dimir is wide open.
Granite Witness: Solid WU common: 55.9% GIH WR on a 3/4 disguise body that draws on flip. It wheeling is a real signal Azorius is open. Good glue for the controlling white-blue deck, though Escape Tunnel beats it for pure fixing. Reliable role-player.
Meddling Youths: A non-premium Boros signpost — 54.8% GIH WR and a negative IWD flag the awkward 5-mana 4/5 haste as a curve-topper you don't build around. Fine if you're in RW, but it's not why Boros wins. Mid-pick playable in the aggressive shell only.
Shady Informant: Replacement-level Rakdos common: 51.8% GIH WR, 6.6 ALSA, and a 35% play rate say it's a late-table filler at best. Black's worst pairing doesn't need a marginal beater. Sideboard/marginal — only a body-count card in a desperate BR aggro deck.
Worldsoul's Rage: Classic splash payoff with a huge spread — 48.2% OH WR but 56.4% gd WR shows it's a late-game finisher you splash for, not an early play. Great as the red top-end in a Golgari-base build. Strong if your mana supports it, clunky otherwise. Mid-pick.
Sanitation Automaton: The format's premier 'glue' two-drop: 54.2% GIH WR and a 72% play rate across every archetype because MKM is starved for good twos. Colorless, high floor, blocks early and chips late. Solid filler that always makes the deck — take it when your curve is thin.
Shadowy Backstreet: Orzhov dual that comes in untapped under conditions and fixes the strong WB pair — 55% GIH WR, 56.8% OH WR. Fixing is at a premium and white is the best color, so this lubricates a top-tier guild. Solid early pick if you're WB-leaning.
Kraul Whipcracker: Underrated Golgari uncommon: 57.4% GIH WR and a standout 4.7 IWD on the deathtouch reach body, with a removal-ish ETB that punishes their board. Best removal-creature BG gets, and it ages well at 5.2 ALSA. Strong playable, prioritize in Golgari.
Enlisted Wurm: 60.6% GIH on a Cascade beater is the real deal — a premium Selesnya/Naya uncommon that's two cards in one body and goes early at 3.7 ALSA. First-pickable in any green-white midrange shell.
Nyx Weaver: 53.8% GIH is honest filler — a sticky reach blocker that mills toward Collect Evidence in grindy Golgari. Fine role-player, but black's the worst color so it rarely overperforms. Solid playable, not a priority.
Hard Evidence: The best blue common is right: cheap card-flow plus a 1/1 body and a Clue, and that 59.5% OH WR shows it's never a dead draw. Auto-include in any blue deck, especially clue/augment shells. Strong early pick.
Victimize: Mythic ceiling, common-level floor — the 52.3% GIH and zero OH WR scream a build-around that needs fodder and a graveyard target, dead in your opener. Reanimation in a grindy Golgari/Rakdos shell only; speculative, not a high pick.
Case of the Gateway Express: Experts nailed it — a 57.7% white go-wide payoff that taps a blocker, doubles as removal, then mass-pumps. Won't wheel, so grab it early in any Boros/Selesnya aggro deck. The flagship color's best non-rare Case.
Karlov Watchdog: 55.6% GIH but a -0.9 IWD says it's all early-game body, no late-game punch — a fine white two/three-drop that fills the curve and enables go-wide. Solid filler in aggressive white decks, never exciting.
Sanctuary Wall: 50.9% GIH and 23.9% play rate mark it as a defensive sideboard card — a Wall that doesn't pressure or close games in a midrange-aggro format. Bring it in versus go-wide, otherwise leave it in the binder.
Case of the Ransacked Lab: 1.7% play rate and no usable data — this rare is a near-unplayable build-around that nobody solved in a clean 2-color format. Sideboard/cut; don't waste a slot chasing the payoff.
Forensic Gadgeteer: A rare that plays like a C-card — 51.9% GIH because it wants an artifact/clue density the grindy guild decks rarely assemble. Playable in a dedicated Ux artifact shell only; otherwise a mediocre body. Mid pick at best.
Reasonable Doubt: A real counterspell that pulls double duty as a Suspect-enabler — the 57.9% OH WR shows it punishes tempo and card-advantage engines. Core Simic/Azorius piece, but cut extras in removal-heavy builds. Strong, situational playable.
Festerleech: That 58.7% OH but 53.0% GD split tells the story — a menace beater that's great on the play, fades when behind. A fine aggressive black two/three-drop, but black's bad color tax keeps it filler-tier. Solid in proactive Rakdos/Dimir.
Outrageous Robbery: Look past the 53.1% GIH — the 48.3% OH vs 55.9% GD gap means it's a grindy card-advantage engine that's dead in your opener. Excellent in a controlling black midrange deck that wants to go long, weak in aggro.
Unscrupulous Agent: Good pedigree dragged down by black — the experts' downgrade is fair, but 55.3% GIH with a built-in discard/Suspect ETB is still a fine playable two-drop. Take it in any black deck; it does real work in Dimir/Rakdos.
Crime Novelist: 49.9% GIH and -2.8 IWD flag it as a treasure/sac build-around that never paid off — clunky and slow for a grindy format. Marginal; only in a dedicated Rakdos artifact-sac shell, otherwise a cut.
Innocent Bystander: 53.1% GIH is a touch above its C grade — a fine aggressive red two-drop whose disguise/flip rummaging adds reach. The -3.2 IWD says it's pure early pressure, so it's filler that fits Boros/Gruul curves.
Suspicious Detonation: The pay-5 mode is a trap — at 51.1% GIH this only earns its keep when you cast it for 2 in an artifact/clue-sac deck. Sideboard removal otherwise; aim for the discount or leave it. Low priority.
Culvert Ambusher: B- grade flatters a 53.3% GIH card — a reasonable green midrange body that flips for value but doesn't move the needle. Solid filler in Simic/Selesnya, nothing you prioritize. Good 20th-card material.
Pick Your Poison: 7.6 ALSA and 10.3% play rate make it explicit — this is a pure sideboard answer for artifacts/enchantments/fliers. Never maindeck it in a removal-rich format; board it in against specific threats only.
Vengeful Creeper: A clunky overperformer — 55.8% GIH on a 5-mana 5/5 that always finds an artifact or enchantment to blow up in this set. Solid green common that doubles as flexible removal; comfortable maindeck in any green deck.
Deadly Complication: 54.6% GIH removal with upside — a flexible Rakdos kill spell that investigates or drains depending on board. The +2.5 IWD confirms it swings games. Strong Rakdos/Golgari playable; one of black's better reasons to be in it.
Ill-Timed Explosion: A genuine bomb — 60.5% GIH and a massive 8.1 IWD on a flexible sweeper-or-Divination that punishes go-wide white. Premium first-pick that defines Izzet and is splashable. Take it over almost anything.
Niv-Mizzet, Guildpact: The 56.2% GIH and 7.4 IWD show it wins when cast, but the 43.1% play rate and WUBRG cost mean it's a hard-to-support payoff in a 2-color format. A strong finisher if your mana enables it; speculative otherwise.
Soul Search: 51.6% GIH and 14.7% play rate — a narrow Orzhov tutor/disruption that rarely makes the cut in a format that wants efficient bodies and removal. Niche sideboard/build-around tool; pass in the main.
Yarus, Roar of the Old Gods: LSV's P1P1 take is well-founded — a 57.0% GIH disguise engine that turns every face-down flip into a free attack trigger. The flagship payoff for Gruul; premium bomb that rewards a disguise-heavy build. Take it highly.
Thinking Cap: Heed the trap warning — 50.5% GIH and -4.4 IWD prove that over-equipping detectives loses games. The 1-mana equip is fine; the cap itself is a marginal card you cut for real interaction. Low-priority filler.
Thundering Falls: A dual land doing exactly what duals do — 55.5% GIH because it fixes and enables splashes, not because it's powerful. Always-playable Izzet mana that smooths any UR or splash deck. Solid early pick for the fixing.
Undercover Crocodelf: 54.1% GIH on a serviceable Simic disguise body — a 2/2 ward {2} that flips into a clue-making blocker. Honest filler that fits the curve in GU; not exciting, but a fine playable common.
Kuldotha Rebirth: A reprint build-around that wants an artifact-heavy go-wide shell MKM never supports; 50% GIH WR and negative IWD confirm it's a non-starter here. Sideboard/unplayable filler.
Evolutionary Leap: No GIH data (too rarely played to read), and a grindy sac-for-value engine is too slow for this removal-rich midrange format. CGB's B+ is over its skis; treat as a marginal Golgari/Simic luxury, not a real pick.
Combine Chrysalis: 47% GIH and a brutal -4.3 IWD say this artifact does nothing the turn it lands; it's a clunky token/disguise engine that needs setup the format won't give you. Ignore CGB's B; this is a trap, sideboard at best.
Gamble: A symmetric/random reprint with no Limited home; 4.5 ALSA just means people open it and pass. Unplayable, correctly an F.
Case of the Pilfered Proof: Numot nails the awkwardness, and the data backs him: 48% GIH and -6 IWD despite 52% play rate means people jam it and lose. The detective payoff rarely solves on time. White-only filler you'd rather not run.
Krovod Haunch: A Food token plus a cheap equip that scales late; 53-54% across the board makes it honest curve-filler and reach for white aggro. Solid role-player, not a high pick, best in Boros/Selesnya.
Seasoned Consultant: Exactly the 2/3 detective body white decks want on turn two, with 55% GIH and a heavy 87% play rate confirming it's a backbone common. Strong playable, great in any white pair (especially Azorius).
Cold Case Cracker: A 3/3 flyer that replaces itself with a Clue on death is premium glue for grindy Azorius/Simic; 55% GIH with positive IWD shows it pulls real weight. Strong playable, happy to trade up all day.
Forensic Researcher: The end-of-format riser is real: 55% GIH and positive IWD even without dedicated collect-evidence support, plus it taps for mana. Strong playable in any blue deck; prioritize over the cheaper detective filler.
Reenact the Crime: 43% GIH and 6% play rate is a damning combo for a rare; the steal effect is too conditional and clunky for this format. Numot's right, it's basically unplayable.
Homicide Investigator: Repeatable investigate off any non-token death for 1B is exactly the grindy value black wants, and 57.6% GIH with +5.5 IWD off a 2-drop is excellent. Premium first-pick that even partly redeems a black deck.
Persuasive Interrogators: A disguise body that investigates on flip; 54% GIH is fine but the low 37% play rate and ALSA 5.9 show it's a replaceable midrange piece. Solid filler for Dimir/Golgari evidence shells, not a priority.
Vein Ripper: 63.6% GIH, +12.9 IWD, ALSA 1.5 — this is the bomb that makes you play black despite the triple-black cost. Build around it. Premier first-pick, full stop.
Demand Answers: Card draw stapled to a sac outlet, but 50.8% GIH and negative IWD show it's pure filler that costs you a card and a creature. Playable when desperate in red; mostly a sideboard/late roleplayer.
Knife: Cheap equipment plus Clue sounds cute but 48% GIH and -5.5 IWD say the body investment never pays in a removal-heavy format. Sideboard; the Clue is the only real upside.
Vengeful Tracker: A two-power detective with reach/relevant text that white-passing red decks happily run; 54.5% GIH and 84% play rate make it a dependable role-player. Solid filler, good early curve piece for Boros/Izzet.
Fanatical Strength: A green combat trick with disguise-flip upside; 56% GIH and positive IWD is strong for a trick, signaling it overperforms in grindy green creature decks. Solid playable, better than its ALSA 6.4 suggests.
Pompous Gadabout: A vanilla-ish disguise body; 52.6% GIH and flat IWD mark it as forgettable midrange filler. Playable if you need bodies in a green deck, but easily cut.
Vitu-Ghazi Inspector: Investigate-on-ETB green common that quietly performs: 55.5% GIH and positive overall WR make it reliable Selesnya/Simic glue. Solid filler, take it as curve-and-Clue density.
Detective's Satchel: 58.3% GIH, +5.2 IWD — LoL is right that the preview underrated it; it's a doubled-up Thopter/value engine that's a real card-advantage motor. Strong playable, premium in Izzet/Rakdos artifact shells.
Insidious Roots: A genuine build-around in a format hostile to them; 51.6% GIH with positive IWD shows it can wheel into a grindy Golgari aristocrats deck but flops elsewhere. Archetype-only payoff, speculative pick at best.
No More Lies: A two-mana Mana Leak with a Clue rider is clean tempo for Azorius; 55.7% GIH and strong OH WR confirm it punishes the curve. Strong playable in WU, situational elsewhere.
Sumala Sentry: A disguise creature that flips into a relevant body; 56.8% GIH and 58% OH WR make it a strong proactive two-drop-ish play for Selesnya. Strong playable, take it highly in white-green.
Cease // Desist: Flexible removal/graveyard-hate split; 54% GIH with positive IWD is fine but the low 27% play rate reflects its narrow Abzan-ish color cost. Solid filler when your colors line up.
Branch of Vitu-Ghazi: LoL called it unplayable but 52.5% GIH says the disguise mode gives it just enough; still, a colorless tapland is a deckbuilding tax. Marginal filler for slower decks needing a board option, low priority.
Undercity Sewers: A Dimir dual that ETBs untapped conditionally and surveils; 55% GIH and +3 IWD reflect the value of fixing plus a tiny dig. Strong land pickup in any UB-adjacent deck, take it early-ish.
Maverick Thopterist: 59.6% GIH and +6.4 IWD for a 5-mana body that makes two flyers and improvises with Clues — this is the Izzet/artifact payoff that overdelivers. Premium uncommon, near first-pick in UR.
Molten Psyche: Constructed-only Wheel-of-Fortune effect; the symmetrical draw helps opponents and the metalcraft damage rarely fires in Limited. No data, F grade, near-zero play rate. Unplayable in MKM draft.
Shard of Broken Glass: A 1-mana +1/+1 equip-2 that mills/Collect-Evidence fuels, but equipment is slow in this removal-rich grind and 8.0 ALSA confirms it's a last-pick. Marginal filler at best, only in a graveyard-payoff Golgari shell.
Quintorius, Field Historian: Disappointing for a gold uncommon: 45% GIH WR and negative IWD say the spell-copy payoff never came together in a creature-heavy format. Sideboard-tier; only consider in a dedicated Boros/Izzet spells build that mostly doesn't exist here.
Crashing Footfalls: Suspend rarely matters in Limited, but two 4/4 Rhinos for the cost still crush at 62.7% GIH and a massive 10.5 IWD. Premium green bomb, slam first-pick in any Simic/Gruul/Selesnya midrange shell.
Case of the Uneaten Feast: The lifegain-on-cast clause needs a fast white go-wide board and the solve condition is awkward; 46% GIH and negative IWD show it underdelivers. A trap rare — leave it for the sideboard.
Make Your Move: Underrated by its 54.7% GIH — LR is right that hitting artifacts, enchantments, and power-4+ creatures answers a huge slice of MKM's bombs and big disguise flips. Strong flexible main-deck removal in any white deck, run 1-2.
Tenth District Hero: 1.4 ALSA and 58%+ GIH: a sticky white two-drop that levels into a removal-immune threat and ends games. Premium first-pick that anchors all four white guilds; exactly the clean efficient body this grind rewards.
Conspiracy Unraveler: A 6/6 flyer is fine, but the convoke-with-evidence cost-reduction wants a build-around that this format mostly punishes; 51% GIH is just a vanilla fatty's floor. Playable Dimir/control top-end, not a high pick.
Furtive Courier: Modest 53.5% GIH — a flexible evasive body that's better when you have artifacts/Clues to power the bonus. Solid Dimir/Azorius role-player, mid-pick filler rather than a build-around.
Steamcore Scholar: 61% GIH and 6.7 IWD: a flyer that loots and refills make it a premium blue rare carrying the spells-matter angle without needing it. High pick in any Izzet/Azorius/Dimir deck.
Hunted Bonebrute: 58.5% GIH with menace and a recursion-via-evidence backside — the donated 3/2 token barely matters when you're attacking for 5. Strong black playable that punches above black's weak guilds; takeable early.
Polygraph Orb: Experts are right: a 5-mana Read the Bones stapled to a feeble ability is far too slow for this grind. The 52% GIH is colorless-inclusion noise; the negative play rate is the real verdict. Sideboard/unplayable.
Anzrag's Rampage: A splashy Constructed rare whose double-strike/destroy payoff doesn't assemble in Limited; 1% play rate and F grade. Unplayable here.
Expedited Inheritance: Symmetrical card-draw enchantment that helps the opponent and demands a sac/aggro engine MKM doesn't support; near-zero play. Unplayable draft card.
Krenko, Baron of Tin Street: 1.7 ALSA, 58.6% GIH — snowballs goblins off every artifact and Clue, and MKM is swimming in both. Premium red rare and a Boros/Izzet/Gruul first-pick that demands an immediate answer.
Aftermath Analyst: A vanilla-ish 53% body whose landfall-recursion ceiling needs a Golgari graveyard/ramp shell to matter. Solid green filler, fine 23rd card, nothing to prioritize.
Flourishing Bloom-Kin: 58.2% GIH and 3.4 ALSA: a green two-drop that ramps and grows with lands, smoothing the curve in any green deck. Strong, reliable playable — best in Simic/Selesnya midrange, take it solidly mid-pack.
The Pride of Hull Clade: Mythic in name only at 50.5% GIH and negative IWD — the +1/+1 counter engine is too durdly for a removal-rich grind that kills the key pieces. Replaceable Simic top-end, not a high pick.
Agrus Kos, Spirit of Justice: 63.5% GIH, 9.1 IWD, 65% on the play — a Boros bomb that turns your detectives/clue-makers into a removal engine. Elite first-pick; the flagship guild's best uncommon-slot payoff and a near-auto-win when it sticks.
Dog Walker: Numot's late read is correct over the early doubters: an X/1 that dodges the toughness-removal gap and makes go-wide dogs, busted at the 11th pick. 57.8% GIH on a common — take it highly in Boros/Selesnya, never cut from white.
Izoni, Center of the Web: 65.3% GIH and a format-leading 14.5 IWD — an absurd Golgari engine that buries the board in spider tokens off Collect Evidence. Bomb-tier first-pick that single-handedly makes the worst guild worth drafting.
Officious Interrogation: The 49% GIH masks a high-variance Fog-plus-Clues blowout that's matchup and pilot dependent; on-play WR is poor because it's reactive. A skill-testing Azorius trick, sideboard-leaning rather than a maindeck staple.
Teysa, Opulent Oligarch: 61.5% GIH, 10 IWD — LR's 'A' is right: an Orzhov snowball engine that grinds out detectives and removal-immune value until you can't lose. Premium first-pick anchoring one of white's strong guilds.
Flotsam // Jetsam: A flexible split card whose 50.4% GIH and Sultai color identity reflect filler status — the gap between OH and GD WR shows it's a reactive value card, not a tempo play. Mid playable in Dimir/Golgari, low priority.
Commercial District: A Gruul dual that enters tapped but Surveils and fixes — 54.7% GIH is solid for a land and the deck-smoothing is real. Strong early pickup if RG/Izzet, take over marginal spells for the fixing.
Underground Mortuary: 55.5% GIH and 2.5 IWD — the Golgari Surveil-dual that also feeds Collect Evidence, the best of the cycle for synergy. Prioritize it in BG/Dimir; clean fixing plus graveyard fuel is worth an early pick.
Treacherous Terrain: 57.1% GD WR shows it overperforms as a Gruul/ramp finisher — cycles for fixing, then dumps a graveyard's worth of damage to close. Strong splashable payoff in RG; note LoL's name/availability caveat. Solid mid-pick reach.
Leonin Relic-Warder: A 2/2 for 1W that exiles a problem artifact/enchantment with upside if it dies; 55% GIH on an 80% play rate is a clean white two-drop that earns its slot in any white deck. Solid filler with real maindeck flex.
Metalspinner's Puzzleknot: Sub-49% GIH and a 19% play rate confirm this is a slow, do-nothing clue rock; the card-draw is too expensive to keep pace in a grindy removal format. Sideboard/marginal at best.
Laid to Rest: 2% play rate and no meaningful GIH sample means nobody touched it; a narrow graveyard-hate enchantment with no maindeck home here. Sideboard-only, and even then rarely worth it.
Tireless Tracker: 60% GIH, +6.2 IWD and a 1.5 ALSA scream bomb: it turns every land into a clue and snowballs card advantage in a format that rewards grinding. Premium first-pick, best in Simic or any green midrange shell.
Defenestrated Phantom: The data backs the experts: 52% GIH on a 34% play rate marks one of the weakest disguise creatures, paying five to flip into a fragile flyer. Filler at most, usually a sideboard card.
Makeshift Binding: Experts call it the best common and the numbers agree: 58.5% GIH, 2.7 ALSA O-Ring removal with lifegain that comfortably beats the ward tax. Premium, near-first-pick removal for any white deck.
Unyielding Gatekeeper: A flexible flicker/bounce body that doubles as tempo removal; 58% GIH with a 1.7 ALSA makes it a quiet workhorse rare. Strong playable, especially good resetting your own disguise creatures or blinking ETBs.
Coveted Falcon: The donate downside is real but the GD WR (53%) far outpaces OH (50%), suggesting it shines later when you can sequence around giving it away. Playable evasive blue body, but a notch below true blue premiums.
Hotshot Investigators: A serviceable five-drop that investigates and pings, but the 34% play rate and 6.8 ALSA show it's a late-table role-player, not a build-around. Filler for Detective/clue decks that want the body and card.
Unauthorized Exit: The 56.9% GD WR confirms LR's read: premium Simic tempo that bounces a flipped disguise creature or blows out a trick, with surveil smoothing draws. Strong playable in any tempo-leaning blue deck.
Illicit Masquerade: Sub-48% GIH and a brutal 46% OH WR expose this as a clunky build-around that does nothing on its own; the format killed cute enchantments like this. Sideboard/unplayable outside a dedicated shell.
Presumed Dead: A cheap reanimation/disguise instant that the 52% GIH and 36% play rate mark as fringe; it needs targets and tempo you usually don't have. Niche filler, mostly for dedicated graveyard builds.
Bolrac-Clan Basher: A solid aggressive red beater with evidence-fueled upside; 54% GIH on an 80% play rate makes it dependable Boros/Gruul material. Solid filler that pulls its weight in any red aggressive curve.
Expose the Culprit: F grade, 47% GIH and a 7% play rate all agree with Numot: a clever disguise-flip trick that demands a density you'll never reliably hit. Cut it; play real cards instead.
Krenko's Buzzcrusher: A 2.0 ALSA artifact bomb that hits hard with relevant abilities, though 53.8% GIH is modest for a rare. Strong playable and a fine pickup, but don't pass premium removal for it.
Airtight Alibi: A green aura that grants ward and a clue; the 54.5% GD WR is fine but the 26% play rate and aura-risk keep it as filler. Reasonable in clue/disguise-protective green decks, otherwise skippable.
Get a Leg Up: Numot's blowout praise is earned: 57% GIH and +1.9 IWD for a cheap trick that can swing combat hugely, plus it makes a clue. One of the format's better tricks, strong in any green deck.
Rope: 46.7% GIH and -4.8 IWD with an 8% play rate make this a trap; the clue equipment is too slow and the body it makes too small. Sideboard/unplayable filler.
Alquist Proft, Master Sleuth: An A-grade bomb whose 58% GIH undersells the late-game inevitability his clue-engine ult provides; +5.4 IWD shows he wins grindy games. Premium first-pick and the Azorius payoff that rewards going long.
Doppelgang: 62.4% GIH and a monstrous 64.7% GD WR with +9.3 IWD confirm LR's A: a scalable Simic finisher that ends games when cast big. Premium bomb-tier in GU, easily worth the early pick despite the C+ grade.
Judith, Carnage Connoisseur: LR nailed it as the biggest underperformer: exactly 50% GIH and a -0.7 IWD because the Rakdos spells shell and black both let her down. Below-rate rare; play her only if Rakdos is wide open.
Private Eye: A 3/3 Detective lord that turns on unblockable evasion off a single clue; 58.7% GIH and +4.6 IWD make it a top WU uncommon, and doubling up is game-winning. Strong playable, premium in Azorius Detectives.
Tin Street Gossip: The Gruul signpost: a 4/4 vigilance that fixes for disguise-flip costs, with a respectable 53.8% GIH. Solid playable that anchors RG, but it's an enabler rather than a power card, so prioritize accordingly.
Fuss // Bother: A flexible split card whose 59.7% GD WR and 88% play rate make it a quiet Azorius all-star, slotting into go-wide flyer decks. Strong playable with real maindeck flexibility across multiple WU builds.
Elegant Parlor: A Boros dual land that fixes and surveils; the gap between 57.6% OH and 53.6% GD WR shows it's best as early mana smoothing. Strong utility land, an easy pickup for the flagship RW deck.
Case File Auditor: A white Detective that digs for your enchantments and Case payoffs; 54% GIH on a 41% play rate marks it as a synergy role-player, not a generic good card. Solid filler that's better in dedicated Case/enchantment builds.
Mystery Key: Card-advantage equipment that the 54.3% OH WR likes early, but the 38% play rate and slow tempo cap it as a clue-engine filler. Playable in grindy blue decks that want extra fuel, otherwise replaceable.
Gnaw to the Bone: Lifegain build-around that the grindy-but-not-lifeloss-driven MKM format has no use for; 7.8 ALSA and a D grade confirm it's a near-pure sideboard card against aggressive Boros. Don't main it.
Smuggler's Copter: Premium colorless P1P1 — 63.4% GIH and 1.3 ALSA are as good as it gets. The looter-on-a-flying-3/3 plays best in white aggro shells that can reliably crew it; just be honest that a grindy Golgari/control build leaves it stranded as the LoL caveat warns.
Monologue Tax: A Constructed-flavored tax enchantment that does nothing on board; F grade and 4.4% play rate are correct. Unplayable in 40-card Limited.
Drown in the Loch: Scaling counter/removal split that needs a stocked opponent graveyard to be flexible — 53.9% is merely fine, and it's stuck in Dimir, the format's worst pairing. Playable when you're in UB, but don't reach; it's a role-player, not a bomb.
Delney, Streetwise Lookout: Despite mythic billing and a 1.7 ALSA, the negative IWD and ~51.7% GIH say Delney is a build-around that mostly underdelivers in draft — you need a real density of small-creature/token triggers to break it. Solid white body, but treat it as a strong filler payoff, not a slam bomb.
Marketwatch Phantom: Quietly excellent white common — 55.4% GIH and a 92% play rate make it a reliable evasive beater that anchors the four strong white pairs. High pick among commons; great in tempo-leaning Boros/Azorius/Orzhov.
Wrench: A clue-equipment that's slow and card-disadvantageous up front; ~51% GIH and 22% play rate mark it as fringe. Fine as a curve-filler in a controlling white deck that wants the eventual investigate value, otherwise a sideboard.
Crimestopper Sprite: The data agrees with the experts' end-of-format demotion — a vanilla-ish 2/2 tempo flicker whose 54% GIH leans on its Collect Evidence body, not its rate. Playable filler in Ux tempo, but take Forensic Researcher and most disguise threats over it.
Intrude on the Mind: Premium blue instant — draw-three plus a 1/1 flyer at 58.5% GIH is exactly the card-advantage engine grindy Azorius/Dimir control wants. First-pick caliber for any blue control deck.
Agency Coroner: Collect-Evidence payoff stuck in mono-black with sub-50% out-of-hand and a 7.9 ALSA — needs a dedicated graveyard shell to do anything. Last-pick filler for Golgari/Rakdos sacrifice, otherwise unplayable.
It Doesn't Add Up: Conditional black removal that whiffs too often (45% OH WR, negative IWD) — the 'only X creatures' clause makes it a trap in the format's worst color. Sideboard/marginal; there are better black cards.
Repeat Offender: A grindy recursive black body that the data pegs as exactly average (51.7% GIH, flat IWD). Fine roleplayer in a Rakdos/Golgari attrition deck, but nothing to prioritize.
Case of the Burning Masks: Strong red uncommon — removal that converts to card draw on 'solve,' which is why 55% GIH beats its modest face value. High pick in Boros/Izzet/Gruul; the solve condition is easy in any creature deck.
Felonious Rage: The data backs LR's end-of-format bump — a 56% OH WR proactive trick that replaces itself with a detective token when the creature dies, so it's never a true 2-for-1 loss. Underrated in aggressive Boros/red; take it higher than the 6.6 ALSA suggests.
Lamplight Phoenix: Recurring 3/3 flyer that's a genuine red bomb — 59.3% GIH and a clean Collect-Evidence recursion loop make it a top pick. Best in graveyard-leaning Izzet/Rakdos; heed the note about not casting under four evidence.
Analyze the Pollen: Green tutor-plus-clue that smooths your bombs and curve — 54.9% GIH with a notable gap toward GD WR (56.2%) shows it shines from hand later. Solid playable in any green midrange, especially Simic/Selesnya building around a key creature.
Glint Weaver: A 7-mana 6/6 lifelink/reach stabilizer that's a backbreaker in grindy green midrange — the 59.4% GD WR confirms 'resolve it and you win.' High pick in Simic/Selesnya/Golgari; one is great, two is deck-defining at the top end.
Rubblebelt Maverick: Premium green one-drop — 55.6% GIH on a 5.5 ALSA is excellent value, and the surveil/self-exile feeds graveyard payoffs. Take it highly in any green aggressive or graveyard shell; it's better than its common rarity suggests.
Anzrag, the Quake-Mole: Gruul mythic finisher that forces blocks and takes extra turns — 56.3% GIH and 5.0 IWD make it a true bomb even in a middling guild. First-pick when you're RG; the untap-on-block loop ends games fast.
Drag the Canal: Efficient Dimir instant — a 2/2 detective body stapled to relevant value at 55.3% GIH with a strong 4.6 IWD is good even for the weak black pairing. High pick in UB tempo; punches above black's reputation.
Kaya, Spirits' Justice: A genuine Orzhov bomb — 60.1% GIH and a massive 9.4 IWD make this planeswalker a first-pick that grinds value and recurs your best creatures. Slam it in any WB deck; one of the format's best mythics.
Rakdos, Patron of Chaos: Format-warping bomb — a 5-mana 6/6 flying trample that draws two or strips two every turn, with a stellar 62.3% GD WR. P1P1 in any deck touching black/red; the rare card that makes Rakdos worth being in.
Tolsimir, Midnight's Light: Elite Selesnya bomb — 63.5% GIH and 10.2 IWD are top-of-format numbers; the lifelink body plus a 5/5 forced-block wolf wins on its own. Premier first-pick; a reason to be in white-green.
Hustle // Bustle: Three-color split that asks for URG and pays off little — negative IWD and Numot's flat dislike confirm it. The format isn't a soup deck, so the fixing cost rarely justifies it; marginal/sideboard.
Escape Tunnel: Believe the experts — the best fixing in the set, and 55.8% GIH on a 94% play rate land is remarkable. Enables splashes and double-pip costs with a real unblockable mode; take it as a near-top-10 common, ahead of where most drafters value lands.
Wojek Investigator: White rare bomb — a 2/4 flying vigilance that blocks everything and draws cards continuously is exactly what the best color wants. 60.3% GIH and 1.4 ALSA make it a first-pick; anchors any white deck.
Stromkirk Captain: Vampire lord that wants a tribal density MKM doesn't reliably support, and Rakdos is a bottom-tier home — the sub-50 GIH WR confirms it underdelivers. Speculative payoff, fine as a late filler in a dedicated Vampire build only.
Consign // Oblivion: Don't be fooled by the C+ grade — 56.4 GIH WR with +3.1 IWD and a 57.5 GD WR is a strong flexible Dimir playable: tempo bounce now, hard removal later. Solid mid-pick despite the worst-color home.
Magmaw: 61.9% play rate and a 4.8 ALSA say players reach for it, but the modest 53.9 WR pegs it as a solid red role-player, not a bomb — a repeatable ping enabler that's better the more sacrifice/evidence fodder you have. Reliable filler.
Field of the Dead: A constructed-only land with zero Limited payoff; you can't assemble enough land diversity here. F grade and no data is correct — never play it.
Doorkeeper Thrull: A rare that whiffs hard: it doesn't even stop disguise flips, leaving a vanilla-ish body at a 51 WR. LR's D/F is on the money — sideboard/unplayable despite the rarity.
Museum Nightwatch: Quietly excellent white common — 55.3 WR at an 82% play rate with a clue attached makes it a true workhorse blocker that fixes flood. Strong filler that always makes white decks; prioritize as connective tissue.
Agency Outfitter: A 6.3 ALSA and sub-51 WR mark it as a clunky blue value creature whose ETB only shines with clue/artifact density. Marginal — playable in grindy Azorius/Dimir builds but rarely worth an early pick.
Cryptic Coat: Unanimous A+: a recastable ward-2 unblockable engine at 63.2 WR, +10.2 IWD, and a near-universal 94% play rate. One of the best cards in the set — premium first-pick that single-handedly takes over games. Snap it.
Jaded Analyst: Numot's right and LR's revision agrees: a 2/3 looting wall is one of the best blockers in grindy Simic, but the 52.3 WR is dragged by aggro decks that don't want it. Archetype-dependent — great in GU, skip in Boros.
Alley Assailant: Solid black common: a reasonable body with reach that posts a respectable 54 GD WR. Nothing flashy, but a dependable role-player to fill out Dimir/Golgari curves. Mid-pick filler.
Lead Pipe: A slow clue-equipment that demands too much setup for its payoff — 50.3 WR and a 7.0 ALSA confirm it's a trap. Marginal; only consider in a dedicated artifact/clue shell, otherwise sideboard.
Rot Farm Mortipede: The +1.4 IWD is the tell — it's a defensive evidence-collector that overperforms when drawn late, but the 7.2 ALSA shows nobody prioritizes it. Niche Golgari filler, fine as a curve-topper in a grindy build.
Case of the Crimson Pulse: LoL likes it as a Rakdos card-advantage engine and a 76% play rate backs the demand, but the flat 53.4 WR and negative IWD say it's a slow, win-more enchantment in a weak guild. Solid but not exciting — playable, not a priority.
Frantic Scapegoat: The 58 OH WR vs 53 GD WR split screams aggressive curve-filler: great on the play, fades in grindy spots. A fine disguise-enabling two-drop for Boros/Gruul tempo, but read the format — it's a role-player, not a bomb.
Offender at Large: A genuinely good red common: 55.6 WR at a common with disguise upside makes it efficient connective tissue for any red aggressive shell. Strong filler — happily maindeck and grab on the wheel.
Archdruid's Charm: Flexible green Charm doing removal/ramp/tutor at a strong 58.1 GD WR — that modal versatility is exactly what grindy MKM rewards. The C+ grade undersells it; treat as a strong green playable worth an early pick.
Greenbelt Radical: Underrated uncommon — 57 WR with a positive IWD and an 88% play rate makes it a premium Simic/Gruul beater whose disguise flip is a board-wide pump. Strong playable bordering on a high pick; the B grade is fair-to-low.
Sample Collector: A 76% play rate and 54 GD WR mark it as a sturdy green evidence/detective engine that quietly overperforms in grindy decks. Solid, always-makes-the-cut filler — better than its unassuming profile suggests.
Assassin's Trophy: The data splits sharply from the B+ hype: 52.1 GIH and a brutal 48.3 OH WR show this catch-all removal lives in bottom-tier Golgari and reads inconsistently. Still premium removal in a vacuum — the low WR is the guild, not the card. Take it, but know your colors.
Etrata, Deadly Fugitive: Mythic that delivers: 58.4 WR, +7.7 IWD, and a recurring evasive assassin engine that grinds Dimir games into the dirt. Strong bomb-tier pick despite living in the worst color pair — first-pick worthy.
Kellan, Inquisitive Prodigy: Elite multicolor rare — 60.6 WR with a 63 OH WR and +7.5 IWD. A 3/4 flyer that draws off your own clue/artifact cracks is exactly the Simic engine the format wants. Premium first-pick that anchors a GU build.
Rakish Scoundrel: A low-impact Golgari common stuck in the format's worst guild; 51.8 WR and a 6.7 ALSA make it pure curve-filler. Marginal — playable if you're in the colors, but rarely worth a real pick.
Treacherous Greed: Conditional Orzhov removal-plus-drain that needs a creature to have died, hence the middling 52.8 WR and low play rate. Decent in a sacrifice-leaning WB build, but the setup requirement keeps it a role-player, not a priority.
Push // Pull: Experts nail it: premium hybrid sorcery where Push is clean early removal and Pull closes games — 86% play rate and 57.3 GD WR back the demand. You want both colors, but in a white-heavy format that's easy. Strong early pick.
Hedge Maze: A Simic dual land that taps for both colors with minor upside — 55.8 WR is mostly the fixing premium, not the card. Solid mana-base pickup for GU/three-color builds; grab it for the fixing, not the body.
Sudden Setback: A reactive blue trick at a 6.4 ALSA and 53.2 WR — fine tempo interaction but narrow, and the negative IWD shows it's situational. Playable role-player in tempo-leaning Azorius/Izzet; nothing to prioritize.
Burden of Guilt: A tap-down Aura at 49.4% GIH and negative IWD — card disadvantage that doesn't kill, classic Pacifism-lite trap. Sideboard/last-ditch filler against a big blocker; never a maindeck pick in a removal-rich format that wants real answers.
Bishop of the Bloodstained: No GIH data and a D+ at 6.8% play means it barely saw a deck — a clunky black bomb-wannabe in the worst color. Treat as a sideboard/late-table piece; the vampire payoff isn't dense enough to build around.
Mistveil Plains: An F-graded utility land at 50.2% — the recursion ability is too marginal and the colorless slot costs you a real spell. Only run it in a heavy-white deck that wants the toolbox; otherwise skip for a basic.
Case of the Shattered Pact: 36.9% play at 52.6% GIH makes this a fixing-on-an-enchantment role-player — splashable mana smoothing that solves into a small payoff. Solid filler for greedy 3+ color builds, but the white guild decks rarely need it.
Due Diligence: A +1/+1 and vigilance Aura that investigates — 51.9% but ugly -2.8 IWD flags the 2-for-1 risk to removal. Fine as a tempo/curve filler in aggressive white (Boros) where you race; not a card you prioritize.
Neighborhood Guardian: 58.3% GIH and a 2.7 ALSA confirm an elite go-wide anthem-flyer — the 61.5% OH means it snowballs from turn three. Premium in any white go-wide shell (WU/WB), an early pick that punishes the format's grindy boards.
Behind the Mask: 49.6% at a near-unplayable 8.3 ALSA — the experts are right that two weak modes don't add up. Pure sideboard tech for disguise mirrors; leave it in the box otherwise.
Curious Inquiry: Numot's right that investigate-on-an-Aura plays better than the 50.5% suggests, especially gluing it to a hexproof-ish face-down body. Still niche filler — only in a dedicated Ux tempo/Private Eye deck, not a default playable.
Living Conundrum: 46.6% GIH and a 42% OH are damning — the build-around that LSV loved never assembled. Don't chase the dream; it's effectively unplayable outside a fully dedicated shell that doesn't exist in this format.
Barbed Servitor: A colorless rare that gets played (73%) but only wins at 51.1% — a fine sacrifice-payoff body that's filler in practice. Take it as a flexible 3-drop in any deck, but it's not a bomb and never first-pick material.
Leering Onlooker: 55.1% with positive IWD and self-recursion is a genuinely strong black uncommon — the rare black card that earns its slot via grindy value. Excellent in Dimir/Golgari surveil-mill shells; a high pick that helps drag the worst color upward.
Slice from the Shadows: 54.2% and +1.8 IWD make this efficient black removal — collect-evidence scales it into a real kill spell. Strong playable in any black deck and a reason to keep some graveyard density; prioritize over most black commons.
Caught Red-Handed: 51.3% at a 7.2 ALSA tells you it's a situational combat/suspect trick, not reliable removal. Solid sideboard or aggressive-Boros filler where you can set up the kill; don't maindeck it as your removal.
Fugitive Codebreaker: 56.1% and a 2.1 ALSA — a hasty prowess threat that flips for value in spell-dense Izzet. Strong playable bordering premium; best in UR where instants/sorceries cheapen the flip, but a fine aggressive body anywhere red.
Person of Interest: The data backs LR's correction over their review — 58.2% GIH and two bodies for four mana make this arguably the best red common. Premium pickup for any red deck, great in Boros go-wide; take it early.
Audience with Trostani: 54.6% GIH but a brutal 50.7% OH and weak card-draw reality (LR's right) make this a build-around trap. Only a late-game value piece in a token-heavy Selesnya grind; not worth the rare slot most games.
Hard-Hitting Question: 57.0% and 3.5 ALSA — premium green removal that fights AND investigates, exactly what a grindy format rewards. Strong first-pickable in any green deck; the kind of clean efficient card that wins MKM.
Sharp-Eyed Rookie: An A-grade engine: 59.2% GIH, monstrous +5.3 IWD, 1.8 ALSA — a 2-drop that snowballs into card advantage and a growing threat. Premium first-pick in green; best in midrange shells with bigger creatures to trigger it.
Aurelia, the Law Above: 60.3% GIH, +6.0 IWD, 1.4 ALSA — a five-mana flyer that draws and pings is a slam bomb in the flagship Boros deck. First-pick-worthy and a signal to be in white-red; close it out by yourself.
Evidence Examiner: 57.8% with a strong +4.2 IWD — a collect-evidence value engine that grinds Simic mirrors. Strong playable and a build-around-lite in GU; rewards graveyard density, so prioritize when your deck can feed it.
Kylox, Visionary Inventor: 44.4% GIH and 5.6% play — LR's F is correct, a seven-drop with self-conflicting requirements that simply misfires. Unplayable; leave this UR rare in the sideboard regardless of how splashy it reads.
Relive the Past: The B+ grade is a CGB outlier the data crushes — 47.9% GIH and a 44% OH show this expensive Selesnya reanimator-value spell underdelivers. Niche sideboard/build-around at best; trust the win rate, not the letter.
Trostani, Three Whispers: 60.5% GIH and a format-leading +7.5 IWD — a 3-mana 4/4 that's near-unkillable in combat and grants evasion. A mythic bomb, premium first-pick that anchors the strong WG/Selesnya shell.
Cryptex: F-graded for good reason: 51.2% but a dismal 48.6% OH because it needs absurd collect-evidence volume to function. A mana rock that doesn't rock — skip it even in dedicated graveyard decks.
Lush Portico: A dual land doing dual-land things — 55.5% GIH and 77% play reflect the value of fixing in a guild format. Strong pick for your colors (especially white pairs); take fixing highly since the two-color decks demand consistency.
Surveillance Monitor: 56.3% with +2.6 IWD — a collect-evidence token engine that's a strong enabler exactly where Numot says (Dimir/Golgari). Strong playable; multiples wheeling signals an open CE lane, so it doubles as a draft read.
